rows.scenario: A fresh conductor enters Phase 7 for this system-altitude session, opens the Phase 7 validation-leg section and Phase 8 close section, derives ConOps, drafts rows, normalizes them, checks them, and prepares the close without reading Fizzy source code.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the phase docs and produced artifacts let the conductor run the validation leg end-to-end from documented steps alone, demonstrating the single-entry-point intent from US-0.

rows.scenario: The conductor runs derive-conops against roadmap/manifest.json and inspects roadmap/conops.md for all manifest user stories and an operational narrative.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff roadmap/conops.md exists with the manifest-derived operational narrative and all story headings from US-0 through US-13, demonstrating refreshed intent binding from US-1.

rows.scenario: The validator reads the derived ConOps and the dogfood digest before judgment, checking that they describe wanted operational outcomes rather than a test inventory.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the ConOps and digest are judgeable as user-intent statements rather than a list of implementation checks, demonstrating operational-intent anchoring from US-2.

rows.scenario: Before closing implementation, the conductor drafts at least one validation row for every active ConOps story with conops_ref, scenario, oracle, evidence_type, and evidence_rationale.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff validation-rows.json contains active rows covering every ConOps story with the required row fields before judgment, demonstrating pre-close row satisfiability from US-3.

rows.scenario: The conductor reviews every dogfood oracle and rejects wording that asks Jason to judge code status, instead using observable user-level outcomes tied to the named story.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the row oracles use observable intent-level outcomes and avoid test-status wording while naming US-4, demonstrating intent-first oracle quality from US-4.

rows.scenario: The conductor assigns each row an evidence type from the approved taxonomy and adds a one-line rationale explaining why that evidence fits the scenario.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff each row has an approved evidence_type and a useful evidence_rationale matched to the scenario, demonstrating evidence taxonomy fit from US-5.

rows.scenario: The conductor assembles one digest batch containing scenario, oracle, evidence summary, and evidence file pointer for every active unjudged row, sized for mobile review.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the digest is self-contained enough to judge the session from mobile without opening the repository, demonstrating one-interaction digest intent from US-6.

rows.scenario: Jason replies to the dogfood digest using the documented row-id verdict grammar, and the conductor parses that reply into row judgments with provenance.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the reply grammar is unambiguous enough for his verdicts to become the intended row judgments with provenance, demonstrating deterministic reply handling from US-7.

rows.scenario: After all rows are judged, the conductor emits system_validation.json, runs self-check, calls the system-validation MCP close tool, and reads card metadata back.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the close artifacts and metadata show a mechanical path from judged rows to system_validation_complete true with documented responses available for rejects, demonstrating close mechanics from US-8.

rows.scenario: The conductor reviews the Phase 8 fail path and dogfood status report, confirming that any failed row would create remediation work, reset the row after resolution, and block close until resolved.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the documented remediation path is actionable enough that a failed validation row would block completion and route to fix work, demonstrating remediation-loop intent from US-9.

rows.scenario: This session, card 5604, runs the validation leg on itself and reaches system_validation_complete true before the session is advanced to completion.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff card 5604 closes through the new validation process with real artifacts and metadata, demonstrating dogfood proof from US-10.

rows.scenario: The conductor runs local self-check on the exact emitted system_validation.json before making the MCP close call and records the clean envelope.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff self-check rejects no issues on the exact emitted artifact before the MCP call, demonstrating local mirror confidence from US-11.

rows.scenario: The conductor confirms no dogfood row was refreshed for convenience and that any future scenario refresh would require the documented human-approved supersede path.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the dogfood ledger shows no hidden row rewrite and the refresh rule is clear enough to prevent agent-approved scope reduction, demonstrating controlled refresh intent from US-12.

rows.scenario: Before digest assembly, the conductor records evidence for every active row so the digest contains execution-backed summaries rather than unsupported narrative filler.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff every active row has an evidence file and evidence_summary before digest assembly, demonstrating evidence-before-digest intent from US-13.
